questions.text: The Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The IGMDP was launched in 1983 under the leadership of Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam. It produced five missiles: Prithvi, Agni, Nag, Trishul, and Akash — collectively covering ballistic, anti-tank, and surface-to-air roles.

questions.text: BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile developed jointly by India and:
questions.explanation: BrahMos is a joint venture between India (DRDO/BrahMos Aerospace) and Russia (NPO Mashinostroyeniya). The name combines Brahmaputra (India) and Moskva (Russia). It is the world's fastest operational cruise missile at Mach 2.8.

questions.text: India demonstrated Anti-Satellite (A-SAT) capability through 'Mission Shakti' in:
questions.explanation: Mission Shakti was conducted on March 27, 2019, when India successfully tested an A-SAT missile that destroyed an Indian satellite (Microsat-R) in Low Earth Orbit. PM Modi announced the achievement, making India the 4th country after USA, Russia, and China to have A-SAT capability.

questions.text: The National Defence Academy (NDA) is located in:
questions.explanation: The National Defence Academy (NDA) is located at Khadakwasla, near Pune, Maharashtra. It is the joint defence training institution for the Army, Navy, and Air Force. The Indian Military Academy (IMA) is in Dehradun.

questions.text: The Agni-V missile is significant because it is classified as:
questions.explanation: Agni-V, with a range of 5,000–8,000 km, is classified as ICBM-class (Intercontinental Ballistic Missile), capable of reaching all of China and most of Europe. It can carry MIRV (Multiple Independently targetable Reentry Vehicles). India officially calls it an 'intermediate range' missile.

questions.text: The Malabar naval exercise involves India, USA, and which third nation?
questions.explanation: Malabar exercise began as an India-US bilateral naval exercise in 1992. Japan became a permanent member in 2015 (previously participated in 2007, 2009, 2014). Australia joined from 2020. The 2021 edition was the first with all four Quad nations participating.

questions.text: INS Vikrant (commissioned 2022) is significant because it is:
questions.explanation: INS Vikrant, commissioned on September 2, 2022, is India's first domestically built aircraft carrier. It weighs 45,000 tonnes and is built by Cochin Shipyard. INS Vikramaditya (2013) was a refurbished Russian carrier. INS Arihant is India's first nuclear submarine.

questions.text: Operation Vijay in 1961 was conducted for:
questions.explanation: Operation Vijay (December 1961) was India's military operation to liberate Goa from Portuguese colonial rule. It lasted just 36 hours. Note: There is also an 'Operation Vijay' in 1999 (Kargil War) — two different operations with the same name, both in different contexts.

questions.text: Vijay Diwas (December 16) commemorates India's victory in the 1971 war. Pakistan's General who signed the surrender was:
questions.explanation: Lieutenant General Amir Abdullah Khan Niazi, Commander of Pakistani Eastern Command, signed the Instrument of Surrender on December 16, 1971 in Dhaka to General J. S. Aurora. Nearly 93,000 Pakistani soldiers surrendered — the largest surrender since World War II.

questions.text: The Indian Air Force was officially established on:
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force was established on October 8, 1932, as the Royal Indian Air Force. The 'Royal' prefix was removed on April 1, 1950, after India became a Republic. October 8 is observed as Indian Air Force Day annually.

questions.text: Kargil Vijay Diwas is celebrated on July 26 to mark the end of Operation Vijay in which year?
questions.explanation: Operation Vijay, the Kargil War, officially ended on July 26, 1999, when Indian forces recaptured all Pakistani-occupied positions in the Kargil sector of Jammu & Kashmir. This date is observed as Kargil Vijay Diwas annually.

questions.text: The Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) post was created in India following recommendations from which committee/review?
questions.explanation: The CDS post was primarily recommended by the Kargil Review Committee (2000), chaired by K. Subrahmanyam, following the Kargil conflict's coordination gaps. The post was finally created in December 2019 and the first CDS General Bipin Rawat was appointed on January 1, 2020.

Akash is a medium-range surface-to-air missile (SAM) system developed by DRDO under the IGMDP programme. It provides air defence against aircraft, helicopters, and cruise missiles at a range of 25–30 km. India has exported it to the Philippines.

questions.text: The India-USA army exercise 'Yudh Abhyas' is a:
questions.explanation: Yudh Abhyas is an annual India-USA Army exercise focusing on counter-terrorism and humanitarian aid operations. It alternates between locations in India and the USA. Vajra Prahar is the India-USA special forces exercise.

questions.text: DRDO (Defence Research and Development Organisation) was established in:
questions.explanation: DRDO was established in 1958 by merging the existing Technical Development Establishment (TDE) and the Directorate of Technical Development and Production (DTDP). DRDO HQ is in New Delhi, under the Ministry of Defence.

INS Arihant is India's first nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine (SSBN), commissioned in 2016. It was built under India's Advanced Technology Vessel (ATV) project. INS Chakra was a nuclear-powered submarine leased from Russia, not built by India.
